reasoning:
The FIDE rating list is updated monthly (on the 1st of each month). On April 13, 2025, Gukesh is ranked 3rd on the April FIDE rating list. For the first resolution date (April 20, 2025), the same April ranking list will apply, meaning his rank will almost certainly still be 3rd, resolving to YES. Therefore, probability for April 20 is very high (0.95). 

For the subsequent dates (May 13, July 12, Oct 10), new monthly FIDE lists will be in effect. Currently, Gukesh is closely trailed by Arjun Erigaisi (5 points behind) and Fabiano Caruana (11 points behind). Because the margins are extremely tight among the top 5, standard rating fluctuations (winning/losing a few classical rating points) could easily see Gukesh drop below 3rd place, or potentially climb to 2nd if Hikaru Nakamura (currently 2nd) drops rating. As the World Champion, Gukesh is clearly elite but rating volatility over half a year introduces significant uncertainty. Thus, the probability of him remaining in the Top 3 gradually regresses towards ~0.50 as the time horizon extends to October 2025.
